title: Feasibility, Alternatives, Risk Experiments, and Stop Decisions · Stakeholder Case: Stop Pivot Evidence
summary: A long capstone plan assumes access to data, users, infrastructure, expertise, approvals, integrations, time, and operating budget that have not been tested. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone stop pivot evidence increment in feasibility proof and alternative decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold.
Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Stop Pivot Evidence · Theory Stop Pivot Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — The plan assumes unavailable data, participants, approval, services, expertise, operating budget, and time while rejecting no-build alternatives. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Stop Pivot Evidence contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Stop Pivot Evidence · Theory Stop Pivot Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold. For Predict Stop Pivot Evidence · Theory Stop Pivot Evidence, keep Stop Pivot Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: The plan assumes unavailable data, participants, approval, services, expertise, operating budget, and time while rejecting no-build alternatives. content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Stop Pivot Evidence condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

options.text: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Persistence despite disconfirming evidence demonstrates capstone excellence. That claim does not survive the predict stop pivot evidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

content.text: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: The plan assumes unavailable data, participants, approval, services, expertise, operating budget, and time while rejecting no-build alternatives. The worked example variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold. A defensible solution applies Stop Pivot Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to feasibility proof and alternative decision record.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-3
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
